perturbation.original_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. Such services may include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc.
perturbation.changed_text: Employees of Federated Advisory Services Company provide support to portfolio managers and other employees of affiliated advisers. Such services may include development of risk management programs, production of portfolio and compliance reports for clients and/or fund Boards, completion of required broker and custody documentation, development and documentation of operational procedures, coordination of proxy voting activities, on-site support of hardware and software, etc.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration', a core function described elsewhere in the agreement (specifically in the RECITALS section) is no longer explicitly listed as a service provided. This creates an in-text contradiction because the RECITALS still state that FASC provides this service, while the amended Exhibit A omits it, leading to uncertainty about whether FASC is still obligated to provide said services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Adviser and FASC have entered into that certain Services Agreement dated as of January 1, 2004 (as amended, the "Services Agreement"), pursuant to which FASC provides certain performance attribution, administration and risk management, equity trading and transaction settlement, fundamental analysis, and quantitative analysis services to Adviser in connection with Adviser providing investment advisory or sub-advisory services to investment companies registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 ("1940 Act") and/or separate accounts not required to be so registered (collectively, "Accounts"); and WHEREAS, the Adviser and FASC desire to amend the Services indicated in Exhibit A to the Services Agreement, solely with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, to provide that, as part of the administration and risk management services provided by FASC, FASC may provide certain coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration services to Adviser when Adviser is acting as adviser or sub-adviser with respect to such Accounts.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration' from the list of services in the Changed Text directly contradicts the RECITALS section (Contradicted Text). The RECITALS explicitly state that FASC provides this service. This omission creates a strong in-text contradiction because it casts doubt on whether FASC is still obligated to provide this service, which is a core function described elsewhere in the agreement. This creates a direct conflict, satisfying the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall be effective as of the date first above written upon its execution and delivery by each of the parties hereto. The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect. The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall be effective as of the date first above written upon its execution and delivery by each of the parties hereto. The Services Agreement, as amended by this Amendment with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act.', the document creates uncertainty. Previously, it explicitly stated that the original service agreement remains unchanged for investment companies registered under the 1940 Act. Now, without this clarification, it is unclear whether the changes outlined in the amendment apply to all accounts or just those that are not investment companies. This is a contradiction because other parts of the agreement refer to 'Accounts' generally, with no explicit exclusion of the 1940 Act registered companies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Adviser and FASC have entered into that certain Services Agreement dated as of January 1, 2004 (as amended, the "Services Agreement"), pursuant to which FASC provides certain performance attribution, administration and risk management, equity trading and transaction settlement, fundamental analysis, and quantitative analysis services to Adviser in connection with Adviser providing investment advisory or sub-advisory services to investment companies registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 ("1940 Act") and/or separate accounts not required to be so registered (collectively, "Accounts"); and WHEREAS, the Adviser and FASC desire to amend the Services indicated in Exhibit A to the Services Agreement, solely with respect to Accounts that are not investment companies registered under the 1940 Act, to provide that, as part of the administration and risk management services provided by FASC, FASC may provide certain coordination of client portfolios and related fixed income trade execution implementation and administration services to Adviser when Adviser is acting as adviser or sub-adviser with respect to such Accounts.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Services Agreement remains in full force without amendment for accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act. The changed text omits this statement, creating ambiguity about whether the amendment applies to these accounts. This omission directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause in the contradicted text, which refers to services provided to investment companies registered under the 1940 Act ('Accounts'). Without the explicit exclusion, the amendment could be interpreted as applying to these accounts, conflicting with the original agreement and creating uncertainty about the applicable terms.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from Pennsylvania to Delaware directly contradicts the established legal framework stated later in the agreement. If other clauses within the original 'Services Agreement' are explicitly tied to Pennsylvania law, those clauses are now in direct conflict with this amendment's selection of Delaware law for governance and construction. This omission creates an in-text contradiction since the older agreement may still refer to specific legal interpretations under Pennsylvania law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Services Agreement also shall remain in full force and effect without amendment with respect to Accounts that are investment companies under the 1940 Act. This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from Pennsylvania to Delaware, directly contradicting the statement in the 'Contradicted Text' which specifies that 'This Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document regarding which jurisdiction's laws apply, potentially rendering clauses unenforceable.
